title: Cyperus correllii
taxonomy: Cyperus correllii is classified within the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family comprises approximately 5,000 species of sedges, most of which are found in aquatic or wetland environments. Cyperus correllii is further categorized within the genus Cyperus, which consists of around 800 species of sedges. The genus Cyperus is a part of the order Poales, which includes various monocot families such as the grasses and rushes. The order Poales is a subgroup of the larger class Liliopsida, also known as the monocots.

intro: Cyperus correllii, also known as Correll's flatsedge, is a perennial sedge species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activity, as well as potential antidiabetic and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Cyperus correllii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to the plant's medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cyperus correllii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are valued for their potential to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
